round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent provided no output in either round; no improvement possible.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Acknowledged other agents' evidence (utility poles, bamboo) in R2, but the reasoning remained largely descriptive of the landscape itself without adding new visual analysis.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Referenced other agents' findings (bamboo fencing, delta ecology) in R2, but the botanical evidence cited was identical to R1.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Incorporated 'utility pole design' and 'wire configuration' from the Meta agent into R2 reasoning, refining the confidence level.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Incorporated 'Landscape and Botanics agents' identification of the delta ecology' into R2, reinforcing the infrastructure-based conclusion.

role_leakage_notes.landscape: Minor leakage: Referenced 'council's observations on utility pole design and bamboo fencing' (Meta/Regulatory domains) to support the landscape conclusion.
role_leakage_notes.botanics: Minor leakage: Referenced 'bamboo fencing noted by the Meta agent' to support the botanical conclusion.
role_leakage_notes.regulatory: Minor leakage: Referenced 'wire configuration noted by the Meta Agent' to support the regulatory conclusion.
role_leakage_notes.meta: Minor leakage: Referenced 'Landscape and Botanics agents' identification of the Ganges-Brahmaputra delta ecology' to support the meta conclusion.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly identified the primary ambiguity (Bangladesh vs. West Bengal, India) and effectively used the tie-breaking evidence from the Regulatory and Meta agents (utility pole taper, bamboo fencing) to resolve the conflict in favor of Bangladesh. The synthesis was logical, evidence-based, and correctly weighted the tie-breakers.
